cards.back: A statistical theorem stating that the distribution of the mean of a large sample of independent and identically distributed random variables will be approximately normally distributed, regardless of the original variable’s distribution.
A range of values within which a population parameter is likely to lie, calculated based on a sample of data and a confidence level.
A measure of the variability or dispersion of a sample mean, calculated as the standard deviation of the sampling distribution.
A value between 0 and 1 that represents the probability that the true population parameter lies within the confidence interval, e.g. 95% CL.
The number of observations or data points in a sample, which affects the accuracy and precision of the confidence interval.
